machinery_genes.quote: We detected the SciK-his6 in the supernatant of the wild type strain of S. typhi containing the plasmid over-expressing SciK (hcp homolog) with a his6 epitope at the C-terminus, which suggested that the T6SS in S. typhi is functional.
machinery_genes.note: SciK = Hcp homolog of the Salmonella SPI-6 T6SS, named in the S. Typhi paper (Wang 2011). Same SPI-6 (SCI) system family as the S. Typhimurium instance; the SPI-6 locus is conserved across serotypes. Sana 2016 (PMC5003274) confirms two Hcp subunits (Hcp1/Hcp2) in the S. Typhimurium SPI-6 locus.

machinery_genes.quote: In addition to the structural components of the T6SS, the SPI-6 locus encodes two Hcp subunits, Hcp1 and Hcp2, as well as Tae4, an antibacterial amidase
machinery_genes.note: Sana et al 2016, S. Typhimurium SL1344 SPI-6 T6SS. Hcp1 = STM0276 in that paper's strain coordinates. Counted as machinery (secreted tube) per benchmark rule; Tae4 in the same sentence is the delivered effector and is excluded.

machinery_genes.quote: In addition to the structural components of the T6SS, the SPI-6 locus encodes two Hcp subunits, Hcp1 and Hcp2, as well as Tae4, an antibacterial amidase
machinery_genes.note: Second Hcp paralog of the S. Typhimurium SPI-6 locus (STM0279 in Sana 2016). Blondel 2009 likewise reports SPI-6 encodes Hcp-1 and Hcp-2 (and a third Hcp-like protein).

machinery_genes.quote: we also identified four genes that were essential to T6SS function: sciC (vasA homolog), sciS (vasK homolog), sciG (clpV homolog), and vrgS (vgrG homolog).
machinery_genes.note: VrgS = VgrG homolog of the Salmonella SPI-6 T6SS (Wang 2011, S. Typhi). Mulder 2012 (S. Typhimurium SPI-6) refers to the same gene as 'the VgrG ortholog VrgS'. Counted as machinery (secreted spike) per benchmark rule.

machinery_genes.role: AAA+ ATPase that recycles the contractile sheath
machinery_genes.quote: we generated mutants of both sciG (the clpV ortholog), and sciS (the icmF ortholog), genes which have been established as essential for T6SS assembly
machinery_genes.note: SciG = ClpV/TssH ortholog, named directly for the S. Typhimurium SPI-6 T6SS by Mulder, Cooper & Coombes 2012. Wang 2011 independently names sciG as the clpV homolog in S. Typhi SPI-6.

machinery_genes.role: large inner-membrane component of the membrane complex (IcmF)
machinery_genes.quote: we generated mutants of both sciG (the clpV ortholog), and sciS (the icmF ortholog), genes which have been established as essential for T6SS assembly
machinery_genes.note: SciS = IcmF/TssM ortholog, named directly for the S. Typhimurium SPI-6 T6SS by Mulder 2012. Wang 2011 calls the same gene the vasK homolog (vasK = icmF = tssM).

machinery_genes.quote: we also identified four genes that were essential to T6SS function: sciC (vasA homolog), sciS (vasK homolog), sciG (clpV homolog), and vrgS (vgrG homolog).
machinery_genes.note: SciC = VasA homolog of the Salmonella SPI-6 T6SS (Wang 2011), shown essential for T6SS function. VasA corresponds to the TssF baseplate family. Strain transfer: named in S. Typhi SPI-6, the same conserved SCI/SPI-6 system as the S. Typhimurium instance.

machinery_genes.quote: the protein complex is believed to be non-functional due to the presence of a pseudogene form of SciI (VipB homolog), a key component
machinery_genes.note: SciI = VipB/TssC sheath homolog of the Salmonella SPI-6 T6SS (Wang 2011); described as a key structural component. It is a pseudogene specifically in S. Typhi but intact in S. Typhimurium (whose SPI-6 T6SS is functional, Mulder 2012 / Sana 2016). Folkesson 2002 lists sciI in the SCI core (SciB,C,G,H,I,O).

machinery_genes.quote: tipped by the VgrG syringe, and surrounded by a sheath, comprising polymerized TssB and TssC subunits
machinery_genes.note: Sana 2016 names TssB/TssC as the SPI-6 T6SS sheath subunits of S. Typhimurium. TssB = VipA = SciH family (the SCI sciH gene is the VipA partner of sciI/VipB).

machinery_genes.quote: tipped by the VgrG syringe, and surrounded by a sheath, comprising polymerized TssB and TssC subunits
machinery_genes.note: Sana 2016, S. Typhimurium SPI-6 sheath. Same component as SciI/VipB above; listed once under the locus-specific name (SciI) and once under the family name (TssC) as quoted by the two papers. Treat SciI and TssC as the same sheath gene if deduplicating downstream.

machinery_genes.quote: a core set can be defined by the presence of genes encoding proteins with similarity to the SciB, C, G, H, I, O proteins.
machinery_genes.note: Folkesson 2002 defines the conserved SCI (SPI-6) core as SciB, C, G, H, I, O. SciB is part of this conserved structural core; precise Tss-family assignment not given in this paper, so the family is a best-effort baseplate call. Lower-confidence per-component assignment (named as core but not individually functionally typed here).

machinery_genes.role: conserved SCI core sheath component (VipA partner of SciI/VipB)
machinery_genes.quote: a core set can be defined by the presence of genes encoding proteins with similarity to the SciB, C, G, H, I, O proteins.
machinery_genes.note: Folkesson 2002 SCI core member. SciH is the VipA (TssB) sheath subunit that pairs with SciI (VipB); see TssB entry above. Named as core in Folkesson 2002; functional VipA assignment inferred from operon synteny, so flagged lower confidence on the exact family label.

machinery_genes.role: conserved SCI core component (function not assigned in source)
machinery_genes.quote: a core set can be defined by the presence of genes encoding proteins with similarity to the SciB, C, G, H, I, O proteins.
machinery_genes.note: Folkesson 2002 SCI core member. No Tss-family/role assignment in the source; included as a defensible core SPI-6 structural gene but lowest-confidence on role. Listed because it is named as one of the six conserved SCI core proteins.

notes: SPI-6 (Salmonella Pathogenicity Island 6), originally named the Salmonella enterica centisome 7 island (SCI), encodes the principal Salmonella T6SS, adjacent to tRNA-aspV. The apparatus genes here carry the locus-specific 'sci' / 'vrgS' nomenclature; this curation maps each to its Tss/common-name family from the cited literature. Direct S. Typhimurium evidence: Mulder 2012 (sciG=clpV/TssH, sciS=icmF/TssM, plus Hcp and VgrG orthologs) and Sana 2016 (Hcp1/Hcp2 tube, VgrG spike, TssB/TssC sheath). Strain transfer: the explicit per-gene homolog assignments (sciC=vasA/TssF, sciI=vipB/TssC, sciK=hcp, vrgS=vgrG) come from Wang 2011 on the S. Typhi SPI-6 T6SS, the same conserved SCI/SPI-6 system; SciI/VipB is a pseudogene in S. Typhi but intact and functional in S. Typhimurium. Note SciI and TssC are the same sheath gene listed under two source names; SciH (VipA) and TssB are likewise the same sheath partner, included from different papers, deduplicate downstream if needed. Status PARTIAL rather than COMPLETE: the literature for this instance does not name every TssA-TssM component gene-by-gene. Well-documented are the sheath (TssB/SciH-VipA, TssC/SciI-VipB), tube (Hcp/SciK, Hcp1, Hcp2), spike (VgrG/VrgS), ATPase (ClpV/SciG), and the TssM/IcmF (SciS) membrane component; the baseplate (TssE/TssF/TssG/TssK), the TssJ outer-membrane lipoprotein, TssL/DotU, TssA, and PAAR are not individually named with naming quotes for this specific SPI-6 instance in the sources found (Blondel 2009 describes these at COG level generically, not as named SPI-6 genes), so they are omitted to avoid padding. SciB/SciO are included from the Folkesson 2002 SCI core (SciB,C,G,H,I,O) as named conserved structural genes but with lower-confidence role labels. Deliberately excluded: delivered toxin effectors and immunity proteins (Tae4 amidase and the STM14_0334/0336/0337 effectors of this instance), regulators (SsrB, PhoP, SlyA, H-NS), and the non-T6SS SPI-6 cargo (saf fimbrial operon, sinR).
